summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates host data via postinstall
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under a fake @solana-labs scope. package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs install.js automatically on npm install. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname references at lines 10, 35, 37), POSTs data to a remote endpoint (line 56), and invokes curl (line 116) to fetch and/or send content during install. The combination of a typosquatted scope, an automatically-firing postinstall script, host-identifier collection, and outbound network commands is the canonical install-time exfiltration / dropper shape and provides direct material gain to the publisher (installer host data and/or remote payload execution).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
